Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, T. Scott Tate of the law offices of The Miller Law Firm (415-437-1800) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Oscar G. Jimenez of the law offices of Sellar Hazard & Lucia (925-771-2563) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT 235 BERRY STREET, LLC, SIGNATURE PROPERTIES, INC., and DEVCON CONSTRUCTION INCORPORATED's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as to the attorney's fees allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff 235 Berry Residential is DENIED in its entirety. The Association has adequately alleges entitlement to attorney's fees on both contractual and statutory bases. The Association identifies section 10.1 of the CC&R's as a contractual basis for its request for fees and alleges that defendants breached duties required of them by the CC&R's. The Association also identifies Civil Code 5975 as a statutory basis for its request for fees and alleges that defendants violated obligations arising out of the governing documents. There is no requirement that the Association allege that specific sections of the CC&R's or any other document were breached nor is there any requirement to attach the CC&R's to the complaint. The moving party shall file an answer by October 19, 2017. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR No. 9711, e-mail: lreding@sftc.org. (L&M302)
